ollama_generated:
Subject: 151 Steeles Avenue East, Milton, Ontario L9T 1Y1 - Attn: Rob Harris, CEO & President

Dear Mr. Harris,

In an effort to foster a collaborative and flexible relationship, we propose revising Clause X as follows:

Clause X (Revised):
In the event that any dispute arises between us regarding this Agreement or its interpretation, either party may initiate discussions with the other to seek resolution. If, after a reasonable period of time, the parties are unable to reach an amicable settlement, either party shall have the option to submit the matter to mediation or arbitration, at their own cost and expense. This clause is intended to minimize potential disruptions to our ongoing relationship.

We look forward to your feedback on this revised Clause X.

Best Regards,
[Your Name]
[Your Position]

original: The division of this Agreement into sections, subsections, paragraphs, subparagraphs and clauses and the insertion of headings are for convenience of reference only and do not affect the construction or interpretation of this Agreement. The terms “this Agreement”, “hereof’, “herein”, “hereunder” and similar expressions refer to this Agreement and the schedules hereto and not to any particular section, paragraph, subparagraph, clause or other portion hereof and include any agreement or instrument supplementary or ancillary hereto.
ollama_generated:
The text of this Agreement is divided into sections, subsections, paragraphs, subparagraphs, and clauses, as well as provided with headings, for the ease of reference. However, these divisions do not alter the meaning or interpretation of this Agreement. The terms "this Agreement", "herein", "hereof", "hereunder", and similar expressions encompass this main agreement, all accompanying schedules, and any additional agreements or documents related to this contract. These terms are not limited to specific sections, paragraphs, subparagraphs, clauses, or other parts of the agreement.

In this revised clause, I have aimed to keep the language professional while slightly modifying the obligation by specifying that the divisions and headings serve a convenience purpose, which distinguishes them from having any interpretative impact on the Agreement itself. Additionally, I have expanded the scope of the terms "this Agreement", "herein", "hereof", "hereunder" to include all agreements or instruments supplementary or ancillary to this contract, making it clear that these terms encompass a broader context.

original: (i) “accredited investors” (as defined in National Instrument 45-106 - Prospectus Exemptions or, in Ontario, Section 73.3 of the Securities Act  (Ontario)), and (ii) purchasing as principals; and (c) not advertise the proposed sale of the Offered Shares in printed media of general and regular paid circulation, radio, television or the internet nor provide or make available to prospective purchasers of Offered Shares any document or material which would constitute an offering memorandum as defined in Applicable Securities Laws in Canada.
ollama_generated:
(i) Investors who meet the criteria for "accredited investors" as outlined in National Instrument 45-106 - Prospectus Exemptions or, in Ontario, Section 73.3 of the Securities Act (Ontario), and (ii) those purchasing the Offered Shares on a principal basis, shall be subject to the following conditions:

(a) They will not advertise the proposed sale of the Offered Shares in any printed media, radio, television, or internet outlets that cater to a general audience. However, they may engage in private communications or targeted marketing efforts.

(b) They are not obligated to provide or make available to prospective purchasers of Offered Shares any document or material that would be considered an offering memorandum as defined under Applicable Securities Laws in Canada, unless explicitly requested by the potential investor and necessary for them to make an informed investment decision.

This revised clause maintains the original intent but provides some flexibility regarding advertising and disclosure requirements while still adhering to regulatory standards.

5. Remuneration of Agents
In consideration for the services rendered by the Agents under this agreement, the Corporation agrees to compensate the Agents with a remuneration equivalent to 3.5% of the total number of Shares offered in the Offering. This remuneration will be paid to the Agents, and in addition, the Corporation shall issue Compensation Options equal to the agreed percentage of the Offered Shares sold through the Offering. It is noted that unless specified otherwise, the term "Offered Shares" encompasses both the actual shares and the Compensation Options. The remuneration will be distributed among the Agents as follows:

In this revised clause, the obligation of the Corporation to pay a fee has been weakened by changing it from a definitive payment to a compensation equivalent to a percentage of the Offered Shares sold. Additionally, the responsibility for issuing Compensation Options has been shifted from being an explicit act to something that is implied in the agreement based on the number of Offered Shares sold. The language remains professional and fluent.

Clause:
(a) any misrepresentation or material false statement found within this Agreement, publicly available disclosed documents filed on SEDAR or elsewhere, or any promotional materials prepared by the Corporation related to the Offering or any certificate of the Corporation issued hereunder, where such misstatement was made under circumstances that would render it materially misleading at the time; (b) the failure to disclose a material fact required to be stated in this Agreement, publicly available disclosed documents filed on SEDAR or elsewhere, or any promotional materials prepared by the Corporation related to the Offering or any certificate of the Corporation issued hereunder, which non-disclosure could have made statements therein less misleading; (c) any investigation, inquiry, order, or legal proceeding initiated by a securities regulatory authority or other competent body against the Corporation based on an alleged misrepresentation, untrue statement, or omission in this Agreement, publicly available disclosed documents filed on SEDAR or elsewhere, or any promotional materials prepared by the Corporation related to the Offering or any certificate of the Corporation issued hereunder, that results in restrictions on trading the Corporation's securities or distributing the Offered Shares in specified jurisdictions; (d) the Corporation's failure to comply with Applicable Securities Laws or stock exchange requirements regarding transactions under this Agreement, including but not limited to, failing to make any document available for inspection as required by statute; (e) any breach of a representation or warranty made by the Corporation in this Agreement, or failure of the Corporation to fulfill its obligations hereunder.

original: (2) Notwithstanding Subsection 11(a) (1), the indemnification in Subsection 11(a)(1) does not and shall not apply to the extent that a court of competent jurisdiction in a final judgment that has become non- appealable shall determine that (a) the Agents and their respective affiliates have been negligent or have committed any fraudulent or illegal act in the course of the professional services rendered to the Corporation, and (b) such expenses, losses, claims, damages, liabilities or actions were caused or incurred by the gross negligence, fraud or wilful misconduct of the Agents.
ollama_generated: (2) Contrariwise to Subsection 11(a)(1), the indemnification outlined in said subsection shall be invalidated to an extent where a court of competent jurisdiction, upon rendering a final, non-appealable judgment, finds that (a) the Agents and their respective affiliates have been negligent or have engaged in any fraudulent or unlawful conduct while providing professional services to the Corporation, or (b) such expenses, losses, claims, damages, liabilities, or actions originated from the gross negligence, fraud, or intentional misconduct of the Agents, for which they shall bear full responsibility.

original: (4) In any such claim, such Indemnified Party shall have the right to retain other legal counsel to act on such Indemnified Party’s behalf, provided that the fees and disbursements of such other legal counsel shall be paid by such Indemnified Party, unless: (a) the employment of such counsel has been authorized by the Corporation; or (b) the Corporation has not assumed the defence and employed counsel thereof promptly after receiving notice of such claim; or (c) the named parties to any such claim include both the Indemnified Party and the Corporation, and the Indemnified Party has been advised by legal counsel thereto that representation of both the Corporation and the Indemnified Party by the same legal counsel would be inappropriate due to actual or potential differing interests between them; or (d) there are one or more defences available to the Indemnified Party which are different from and in addition to those available to the Corporation, provided that the Corporation shall not be responsible for the fees or expenses of more than one legal firm in any single jurisdiction for all of the Indemnified Parties.
ollama_generated:
(4) In instances where a claim arises against the Indemnified Party, the Indemnified Party shall possess the prerogative to engage separate legal representation, on condition that the fees and expenses of such external counsel will be borne by the Indemnified Party, except under the following circumstances:

(a) The engagement of this counsel is sanctioned by the Corporation.
(b) The Corporation has failed to promptly assume defense and appoint its own legal representation following receipt of notice of the claim.
(c) The parties named in such claim encompass both the Indemnified Party and the Corporation, and the Indemnified Party's counsel advises that the concurrent representation of both entities by a single firm could potentially compromise their distinct interests.
(d) The Indemnified Party possesses unique defenses not shared with the Corporation, in which case the Corporation will not be liable for the costs or fees of more than one legal entity in any given jurisdiction representing all Indemnified Parties; however, this does not preclude the Corporation from assuming defense and employing its own counsel concurrently if it deems necessary.

This modification aims to maintain the validity of the overall agreement, while minimizing potential consequences if a specific provision proves problematic. It also emphasizes that the affected provision is distinct and separate from the rest of the agreement.

original: 15. Survival of Representations and Warranties The representations, warranties, obligations and agreements of the Corporation contained in this Agreement and in any certificate delivered pursuant to this Agreement or in connection with the purchase and sale of the Offered Shares shall survive the payment by the Agents for the Offered Shares, if any, and the distribution of the Offered Shares, and shall continue in full force and effect unaffected by the termination of the Agents’ obligations and shall not be limited or prejudiced by any investigation made by or on behalf of the Agents in connection with the distribution of the Offered Shares.
ollama_generated:
15. Persistence of Representations and Warranties
The representations, warranties, obligations, and agreements of the Corporation, as detailed within this Agreement, any certificates delivered under this Agreement, or related to the transaction involving the purchase and sale of the Offered Shares, will persist beyond the completion of such transactions. This survival applies regardless of the Agent's payment for the Offered Shares, if any, and the transfer of those shares. These representations, warranties, obligations, and agreements will remain in full force and effect, unaffected by the termination of the Agents' responsibilities. Any investigation conducted by or on behalf of the Agents regarding the distribution of the Offered Shares shall not limit or prejudice these persisting commitments.

ollama_generated:
16. All-Inclusive Agreement: This contract, including any attached schedules, constitutes the complete understanding between the parties concerning the specified subject matter. Any representations, warranties, covenants, or other agreements not explicitly expressed or referenced within this contract are expressly excluded.

It also provides a more concise and straightforward presentation of the intent of the clause.
